> ### Power Analysis
>
> A simulation-based power analysis was conducted to determine the required sample size. For extraneous cognitive load and acceptability, we used cumulative link mixed models (CLMM) and assumed a moderate effect size (d = 0.5) with Benjamini-Hochberg correction. For comprehension, we framed the hypothesis as non-inferiority, testing whether the condensed format produces accuracy not more than 7 percentage points below the traditional format (baseline = 85%), using a one-sided α = 0.05.
>
> We simulated 1,000 datasets at each sample size from N = 50 to N = 300, sampling from a population of 10,000 simulated participants. Results indicated that **N = 125** provides ≥80% power for all primary outcomes: detecting moderate superiority effects on cognitive load and acceptability, and demonstrating non-inferiority on comprehension assuming the formats are equivalent.
